memory.summary: My plan to finish the assignment analysis first is at risk because cohort_3 is inviting me to lunch, and I'm worried about missing their social time.

desires: Not look like I'm ditching the lunch with cohort_3, because they're my closest friends and I hate the tension of being late or absent.
Actually finish the assignment analysis by 6 PM so I don't have to scramble later when my focus is already shot from the sleep debt.
Get through this afternoon without snapping at anyone or having a panic attack about the conflicting demands.

goals.description: Go eat lunch with cohort_3 immediately to avoid the social tension of ditching them, then attempt a quick review of the assignment analysis if time permits.
goals.rationale: My conflict avoidance means I cannot handle the stress of being late or absent from my closest friends (cohort_3). Even though finishing the assignment is an academic priority, the immediate emotional cost of social friction outweighs it in this short planning horizon. I'll try to multitask or just do a surface-level check later.

goals.description: Finish the assignment analysis by 6 PM to stop worrying about it and preserve mental energy for the evening.
goals.rationale: I know I need to finish this before my focus drops completely due to sleep debt, but right now the urge to be with friends is stronger. I'm deferring this because my impulsive nature wants immediate relief from social anxiety first; I'll tackle the 'soft' hardness task once the lunch rush passes or if I feel guilty enough during dinner.

agent.last_reasoning: Going with cohort_3 avoids the immediate friction of ditching them, which aligns with my conflict avoidance. I'll eat quickly and scan the assignment later; the social cost of being late right now outweighs the academic gain of starting early.
